title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh herbs and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, comforting flavors of this traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup, a hearty and healthy meal for any day.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, cherished for its comforting warmth and rich, earthy flavors. This hearty and nutritious soup is a staple in Turkish homes, perfect for any season. Its smooth texture and aromatic spices make it an irresistible and satisfying meal for the whole family.

ingredients.notes: or to taste, for the soup

instructions.title: Prepare the Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot, sautéing until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste, dried mint, ground cumin, and 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and deepen the flavor of the tomato paste.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Broth
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot and stir to combine with the aromatic mixture. Pour in the vegetable or chicken broth. Season with salt and black pepper.

instructions.title: Simmer to Perfection
instructions.text: Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend for Smoothness
instructions.text: Carefully remove the pot from heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Mint-Butter Topping
instructions.text: While the soup simmers or after blending, melt butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Add 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes and a pinch of dried mint. Cook for 30 seconds until fragrant and the butter is slightly browned.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a spoonful of the sizzling mint-butter topping. Garnish with fresh mint leaves or serve with lemon wedges on the side for a tangy finish.

tips: For an extra velvety texture, pass the blended soup through a fine-mesh sieve after blending, though it's usually not necessary for Mercimek Corbasi.
Adjust the amount of red pepper flakes in the butter topping to your spice preference; a generous amount adds a beautiful color and warmth.
Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day! Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days and reheat gently on the stovetop.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Turkey, where warmth and hospitality are often served in a bowl. Among the countless treasures of Turkish cuisine, one dish stands out for its comforting embrace and vibrant flavor: Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This isn't just a soup; it's a staple, a symbol of home, and a testament to the simple yet profound deliciousness that can be created with humble ingredients. Whether you're seeking a hearty starter, a nourishing lunch, or a soothing meal for any time of day, Mercimek Corbasi delivers a burst of earthy goodness that will transport your taste buds straight to the bustling markets and cozy kitchens of Turkey.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi is a creamy, flavorful, and incredibly comforting soup made primarily from red lentils. Its signature vibrant orange-red hue and smooth, velvety texture make it instantly recognizable. More than just visually appealing, this soup is a powerhouse of flavor, often characterized by a delicate balance of earthy lentils, aromatic spices like dried mint and cumin, and a subtle tang from tomato paste and a generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice.

A cornerstone of Turkish culinary tradition, Mercimek Corbasi is enjoyed year-round, from a light breakfast to a hearty dinner. It’s a common sight on restaurant menus across Turkey, often served as the first course of almost any meal. Beyond its delicious taste, it's also remarkably nutritious, packed with protein, fiber, and essential minerals, making it a wholesome choice for any diet. Its simplicity in preparation belies its profound depth of flavor, making it a favorite for both experienced cooks and beginners alike.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Crafting an authentic bowl of Mercimek Corbasi requires a few key ingredients, most of which are readily available. The magic lies in the quality of these components and how they are brought together.

For the Soup Base:
*   **Red Lentils:** 1 cup (200g) – The star of the show. Ensure they are rinsed thoroughly.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, chopped – Provides a sweet, aromatic foundation.
*   **Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, minced – Adds depth and pungency.
*   **Tomato Paste:** 1 tablespoon – For color, richness, and a hint of umami.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** 2 tablespoons – For sautéing the aromatics.
*   **Flour (Optional):** 1 tablespoon – Some recipes use this for extra thickening; others omit it for a naturally thickened soup.
*   **Vegetable or Chicken Stock:** 6-7 cups (1.5-1.75 liters) – Or water, though stock adds more flavor.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste.

For the Spices:
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** 1 teaspoon – Essential for that authentic Turkish flavor.
*   **Cumin:** ½ teaspoon – Enhances the earthiness of the lentils.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber or Maras Pepper):** ½ - 1 teaspoon, or to taste – For a gentle warmth and characteristic Turkish spice.

For Garnish (Crucial for Serving):
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Absolutely essential for brightening the soup.
*   **Extra Dried Mint & Red Pepper Flakes:** For the optional, but highly recommended, chili-mint butter drizzle.
*   **Butter:** 1 tablespoon – For making the flavorful "nane yagi" (mint oil).

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Mercimek Corbasi

Making this delightful Turkish Red Lentil Soup is a straightforward process, yielding incredibly rewarding results.

1.  **Rinse the Lentils:** Place the red lentils in a fine-mesh sieve and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. Set aside.

2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Flour (Optional):** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly. Cooking the tomato paste briefly deepens its flavor. If using flour, sprinkle it over the mixture and cook for another minute, stirring to combine and cook out the raw flour taste.

4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquids:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot. Pour in the vegetable or chicken stock (or water). Stir well to combine everything.

5.  **Season and Simmer:** Add the dried mint, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

6.  **Blend Until Smooth:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don’t have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (ensure the lid is vented for hot liquids) and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot stock or water until it reaches your desired consistency.

7.  **Prepare the Chili-Mint Butter (Nane Yagi):** This step is optional but highly recommended for an authentic finish. In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the extra dried mint and red pepper flakes. Cook for about 30 seconds to 1 minute, until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices. Remove from heat immediately.

8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a spoonful of the fragrant chili-mint butter and serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice before eating is crucial for balancing the flavors.

## Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Turkish Red Lentil Soup is easier than you think with these expert tips:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** Don't skip rinsing. It's vital for a clear, vibrant soup and helps prevent a gummy texture.
*   **Cook the Tomato Paste:** Briefly sautéing the tomato paste before adding liquids enhances its flavor and reduces its raw acidity.
*   **Blend for Silkiness:** An immersion blender is your best friend here. Blend until absolutely no lumps remain for that signature velvety smooth texture.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Lemon:** Fresh lemon juice is not just a garnish; it brightens the entire soup, cutting through the richness and elevating all the flavors. It's non-negotiable for an authentic taste.
*   **The Nane Yagi (Mint Butter):** This chili-mint butter drizzle is the crowning glory. It adds an aromatic, slightly spicy, and visually appealing finish that truly defines the soup.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils absorb liquid, so your soup might thicken as it cools. Feel free to thin it out with a splash of hot water or stock when reheating or if it's too thick after blending.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Mercimek Corbasi

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and pairs well with a variety of accompaniments, making it suitable for different meal occasions.

*   **With Crusty Bread:** The most classic pairing. Serve with fresh, crusty Turkish bread (pide), a sesame-crusted simit, or even a simple baguette for dipping.
*   **As a Starter:** It's traditionally served as the first course of a larger Turkish meal, preparing the palate for what's to come.
*   **Light Lunch:** Enjoy a generous bowl on its own for a satisfying and nutritious lunch.
*   **Alongside a Salad:** A simple shepherd's salad (çoban salatası) with finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and fresh herbs, dressed with olive oil and lemon, offers a refreshing contrast.
*   **Extra Toppings:** Beyond the lemon and chili-mint butter, some people enjoy a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or a dollop of plain yogurt (though less traditional for this particular soup) for added creaminess.

## Variations and Substitutions for Red Lentil Soup

While the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** The standard recipe is easily made vegan by using olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and vegetable stock instead of chicken stock.
*   **Adding Vegetables:** For extra nutrition and flavor, you can sauté finely diced carrots and potatoes along with the onion and garlic. They will soften and blend seamlessly into the soup, adding a subtle sweetness and body. A small amount of red bell pepper can also be added.
*   **Spice Variations:** While dried mint and cumin are essential, you can experiment with other spices. A pinch of sumac can add more tang, or a dash of sweet paprika can enhance the color and flavor. Aleppo pepper can be used for a fruitier, milder heat than standard red pepper flakes.
*   **Thickening Agents:** If you prefer a thicker soup without using flour, simply cook the lentils a bit longer and blend thoroughly. The natural starch from the red lentils will provide plenty of body.
*   **Garnish Innovations:** Beyond the traditional, consider crispy fried onions, toasted pumpkin seeds, or even a sprinkle of crumbled feta cheese (though not traditionally Turkish) for textural contrast.

In conclusion, Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's an experience—a warm, inviting bowl that embodies the soul of Turkish cooking. Its simplicity, nutritional value, and profound deliciousness make it a dish you'll want to revisit again and again. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be enchanted by the comforting magic of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup. Afiyet olsun! (Bon appétit!)
